2605.00435.title: Escaping Mode Collapse in LLM Generation via Geometric Regulation
2605.00435.paper_summary: Mode collapse is a persistent challenge in generative modeling and appears in autoregressive text generation as behaviors ranging from explicit looping to gradual loss of diversity and premature trajectory convergence. We take a dynamical-systems view and reinterpret mode collapse as reduced state-space accessibility caused by *geometric collapse*: during generation, the model's internal trajectory becomes confined to a low-dimensional region of its representation space. This implies mode collapse is not purely a token-level phenomenon and cannot be reliably solved by symbolic constraints or probability-only decoding heuristics. Guided by this perspective, we propose *Reinforced Mode Regulation* (RMR), a lightweight, online state-space intervention that regulates dominant self-reinforcing directions in the Transformer value cache (implemented as low-rank damping). Across multiple large language models, RMR substantially reduces mode collapse and enables stable, high-quality generation at extremely low entropy rates (down to 0.8 nats/step), whereas standard decoding typically collapses near 2.0 nats/step.
2605.00435.paper_summary_zh: 模式崩潰是生成建模中的一個持續挑戰，在自回歸文本生成中表現為從明顯的循環到逐漸喪失多樣性和過早的軌跡收斂等行為。<lb/>我們採取動態系統的視角，並將模式崩潰重新解釋為由*幾何崩潰*引起的狀態空間可達性降低：在生成過程中，模型的內部軌跡被限制在其表示空間的低維區域內。<lb/>這意味著模式崩潰並不僅僅是一個標記級別的現象，並且無法通過符號約束或僅基於概率的解碼啟發式方法可靠地解決。<lb/>在這一觀點的指導下，我們提出了*增強模式調節*（RMR），這是一種輕量級的在線狀態空間干預，調節Transformer值緩存中的主導自我增強方向（實現為低秩阻尼）。<lb/>在多個大型語言模型中，RMR顯著減少了模式崩潰，並在極低的熵率（低至0.8 nats/step）下實現穩定、高質量的生成，而標準解碼通常在接近2.0 nats/step時崩潰。
2605.00435.author: Xin Du et.al.
